km.France: ប្រទេសបារាំង

zh.lifeSummary: Thomas De KABE於1050年出生在法國。他和Mrs Thomas de Kabe至少有1個兒子。他去世在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Thomas De KABE于1050年出生在法国。他和Mrs Thomas de Kabe至少有1个儿子。他去世在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: KABE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the personal name Baba (from Czech baba ‘grandmother, old woman’). This name was sometimes given as an omen or well-wishing name to a tenderly guarded or seriously sick child, in the hope of prophesying a long life.
Germanized form of Sorbian Baba or Babo, or of some other Slavic nickname derived from baba ‘(old) woman’ (compare 1 above).
German: probably a variant of   Baab  , a name derived from baby talk.

variants.name: Baba
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Takashi, Tetsuo, Seiji, Tsutomu, Akifumi, Arata, Ayako, Haruo, Haruyo, Hideo, Hikari, Hiroaki.  Arabic/Muslim Abbas, Akram, Ali, Bader, Daouda, Essam, Fadi, Fawaz, Faysal, Fuad, Hajji, Idrees.

Japanese: written 馬場 ‘horse-riding ground, race track’. This is a common placename. This surname is found mostly in west central Japan. One Baba family in Kai (now Yamanashi prefecture) were samurai, vassals of the   Takeda   family.
Muslim: from Arabic bābā, a diminutive of abū ‘father’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas De KABE was born in 1050, in France. He had at least 1 son with Mrs Thomas de Kabe. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas De KABE est né en 1050, à France. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mrs Thomas de Kabe. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas De KABE wurde 1050 in Frankreich geboren. Er hatte mit Mrs Thomas de Kabe mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas De KABE è nato il nel 1050 nel Francia. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mrs Thomas de Kabe. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas De KABEは1050年にフランスで生まれた。彼はMrs Thomas de Kabeとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas De KABE(은)는 1050년 프랑스에서 태어났다. 그는 Mrs Thomas de Kabe(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Thomas De KABE nasceu em 1050, em França. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mrs Thomas de Kabe. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas De KABE родился в 1050 г. в местности Франция. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mrs Thomas de Kabe. Он умер в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Thomas De KABE nació en 1050, en Francia. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Mrs Thomas de Kabe. Murió en su pueblo.
